Unrestricted web searches surfaced only unrelated results (a video-game character and a Galician verb form), suggesting Ancoran is a very early-stage, pre-launch or stealth company with no public web footprint at this time. Based on the conference data, it operates in the Cloud Infrastructure & Data Centers space.
company_goals: Company goals could not be grounded in any public source. Given its Cloud Infrastructure & Data Centers classification, Ancoran is presumably building or providing cloud/data-center infrastructure, but no products, mission, target customers or use cases could be confirmed from available web sources.
funding_notes: No reliable funding data found for an entity named exactly 'Ancoran'; search returned unrelated companies (Ancora, Ankored, Anrok).

title: Sales Director - New Business
person_info: Alex Webb is Sales Director for New Business at Stellanor Datacenters, where he drives commercial growth for the company's UK colocation portfolio. He joined Stellanor through its acquisition of Redcentric's data centre business, having previously worked on the sell-side of that deal at Redcentric. His background is in data centre and connectivity sales across the UK market.
company_info: Stellanor Datacenters is a UK-based, DWS-backed data centre operator launched in 2025 that delivers sustainable regional colocation services for enterprises, IT and managed cloud service providers, and public/government organisations. Per its homepage, it offers colocation, carrier-neutral connectivity, and migration/hybrid-cloud services from facilities running on 100% green energy with 24/7 support. It now operates around 10-12 sites across the UK (London, Cambridge, Gatwick, Reading, Woking, West Yorkshire and more) after acquiring eight data centres from Redcentric and earlier assets from Colt.
company_goals: Stellanor is building a new generation of regional, edge-focused, AI-ready data centres positioned for proximity, performance, sustainability and accessibility. It aims to become the leading platform of regional data centres serving national and international enterprises and service providers needing wholesale and AI inference capacity, with planned expansion across the UK and the Nordics. Its customers are enterprises, cloud/managed service providers, academic institutions and public-sector bodies who choose their own connectivity providers and value sovereign, green, low-latency infrastructure.
total_funding: Backed by DWS (institutional, amount not disclosed)
latest_round: Acquisition financing (8 data centres from Redcentric), up to GBP127M enterprise value, completed May 2026
key_investors: DWS Group (infrastructure fund)
funding_notes: Not a VC-funded startup; UK urban data centre platform launched 2025, capitalized via DWS-managed infrastructure fund; grew 2 to 11 sites via acquisitions.

title: Principal AI Advisor
person_info: Jianhe Liao serves as Principal AI Advisor at Bitdeer, advising on the company's expansion into AI cloud and high-performance computing. The role sits within Bitdeer's AI organization, which spans its AI Cloud business and frontier Bitdeer AI Lab. (No definitive public LinkedIn profile was confirmed; this individual is distinct from Bitdeer's departing CFO Jianchun Liu.)
company_info: Bitdeer Technologies Group (NASDAQ: BTDR) is a Singapore-headquartered, vertically integrated technology company for Bitcoin mining and AI/high-performance computing. Per its homepage, it spans the full value chain: in-house IC design and SEALMINER mining-rig manufacturing, data center construction and operations, cloud mining, and a fully integrated AI cloud. Its AI cloud is powered by thousands of NVIDIA GPUs (GB200 NVL72, B200, H200/H100, and upcoming GB300/B300) delivering compute for model training, inference and scaling.
company_goals: Bitdeer is building out a global AI infrastructure footprint, expanding from Southeast Asia into the US and Norway, while continuing to scale its self-mining hashrate and SEALMINER hardware sales. It is increasingly converting and repurposing its roughly 3 GW global power capacity (US, Norway, Bhutan, Canada, Malaysia, Ethiopia) toward AI cloud and colocation workloads. It serves Bitcoin miners (retail and institutional), hosting customers, and AI/ML developers needing turnkey GPU compute, positioning compute, energy and chip design as one integrated platform for the AI era.
key_investors: Tether (~25% stake, $100M+), Cantor Fitzgerald
funding_notes: Nasdaq-listed (BTDR) Bitcoin mining/HPC firm; went public via 2023 SPAC; funds growth via convertible note raises ($150M 2024, $360M 2024, $330M Jun 2025) plus Tether placement.

title: Director
person_info: Stephen Smith is a Director at vCluster (vCluster Labs, formerly Loft Labs), the company behind the open-source vCluster virtual Kubernetes project. In a director-level commercial/go-to-market capacity he supports the company's enterprise and AI-cloud customers adopting virtual Kubernetes clusters. (No single confirmed public LinkedIn profile was isolated for this exact individual.)
company_info: vCluster (vCluster Labs / Loft Labs, San Francisco) builds the open-source vCluster technology that lets engineers spin up fully functional, isolated virtual Kubernetes clusters that run inside a namespace of an underlying cluster or on dedicated/bare-metal infrastructure. Per its homepage, each tenant gets its own API server, CRDs and RBAC with a cluster experience indistinguishable from a dedicated Kubernetes cluster, virtualizing the control plane for strong multi-tenant isolation. The project is a CNCF-certified Kubernetes distribution with tens of millions of virtual clusters created and adoption by NVIDIA, Adobe, CoreWeave, JPMorganChase and 50+ GPU clouds.
company_goals: vCluster aims to give every tenant their own isolated Kubernetes cluster and let operators run Kubernetes 'like a hyperscaler,' targeting AI cloud providers, enterprise AI factories, sovereign clouds, and multi-cloud platform teams. It is building out related products (vNode for runtime isolation, vMetal to turn GPU racks into a cloud platform, managed Slurm) to provide a complete foundation for production AI infrastructure and 100K-GPU superclusters. Core use cases include per-customer tenant clusters for software vendors, isolated GPU environments per ML team, cost consolidation/sleep mode, and hybrid Kubernetes standardization across EKS/GKE/AKS and bare metal.
key_investors: Khosla Ventures, Fusion Fund, Surface Ventures, Emergent Ventures, Berkeley SkyDeck Fund
funding_notes: Operated by Loft Labs; creators of open-source virtual Kubernetes clusters (vCluster).

title: Associate Director, Commercial
person_info: Jonathan Taylor is Associate Director, Commercial at IREN, based in the San Francisco Bay Area, where he works on the commercial side of the company's vertically integrated AI Cloud and data center business. He recently joined IREN after an 11-year career at Kearney, with earlier experience at McKinsey & Company and Gap Inc., and holds an MBA from Stanford Graduate School of Business. He describes himself as a seasoned executive in energy, infrastructure and emerging technology.
company_info: IREN Limited (NASDAQ: IREN; formerly Iris Energy, founded 2018, Sydney) operates next-generation, 100% renewable-powered data centers purpose-built for AI, high-performance computing and Bitcoin mining. Per its homepage, it owns and operates its electrical infrastructure and facilities end-to-end, offering AI Cloud (GPU clusters on NVIDIA reference architecture), colocation and build-to-suit infrastructure. It runs six North American sites (British Columbia and Texas, plus Oklahoma in development) with about 810 MW operational and ~5 GW of power secured.
company_goals: IREN is rapidly transforming from a Bitcoin miner into a major AI data center player, building grid-connected, power-dense, renewable-powered capacity and scaling its GPU fleet (expanding toward 150,000 GPUs with NVIDIA B300 systems) under major contracts including Nvidia and Microsoft. It is expanding aggressively, including the 2,000 MW Sweetwater (Texas) and Oklahoma campuses and international moves such as Spain (Nostrum) and Australia (Bundey). It serves AI labs and enterprises needing fast, scalable GPU compute, plus customers wanting turnkey data centers or build-to-suit AI-ready facilities, differentiating on vertical integration and construction discipline.
latest_round: NASDAQ-listed (IPO Nov 2021); ongoing ATM/equity & GPU financing
key_investors: Public/institutional investors; NYDIG (equipment financing)
funding_notes: Formerly Iris Energy; NASDAQ:IREN Bitcoin miner pivoting to AI cloud; FY2025 revenue $501M; $9.7B Microsoft AI cloud contract.

title: COO
person_info: Guillaume Levy is Chief Operating Officer (Directeur des operations) at AntemetA, based in the greater Paris region, where he leads operations for the French cloud and IT services group. He joined AntemetA after about seven years at OVHcloud and earlier involvement with Time for the Planet, and holds an MBA from the Conservatoire National des Arts et Metiers. He is publicly active around AntemetA's sovereign-cloud and cyber-resilience messaging (data hosted exclusively in France, DORA/NIS2/GDPR compliance).
company_info: AntemetA is a French private managed cloud and on-premises IT services provider, founded in 1995 and headquartered in Guyancourt, France, with operations in France and Morocco. Per its homepage, it organizes its expertise around five pillars: network, infrastructure, cybersecurity, data management, and platforms & solutions (including SAP). It runs ISO 27001 / HDS / ISAE 3402-certified data centers in France and is recognized by Gartner as a representative vendor for managed Backup-as-a-Service, serving clients such as Alten, Engie IT, Wolters Kluwer and Groupe Beaumanoir.
company_goals: AntemetA is building out sovereign, secure French cloud offerings centered on data protection, cyber-resilience and disaster recovery, with a focus on regulatory compliance (GDPR, NIS2, DORA, CRA) and data hosted exclusively in France. It delivers managed services such as Backup-as-a-Service, mutualized SOC, automated/disaster-recovery cloud (e.g. on HPE GreenLake) and SAP-as-a-Service, increasingly augmented with AI for threat detection. Its target customers are mid-to-large French and European enterprises and public institutions seeking proximity, sovereignty, security and outsourced management of critical IT infrastructure.
funding_notes: Established French IT infrastructure/cloud SME founded 1995 (~300 employees); privately held with no disclosed venture funding.

title: Director
person_info: Michael Dippenaar is Global Sales & Client Success Director at TecEx (with the company since 2020, in the director role since Jan 2022), where he helps enterprise clients navigate cross-border IT procurement, VAT, logistics and compliance and has played a key role in closing technology deals exceeding $950M. He is an admitted Attorney of the High Court of South Africa with a BCom in Law and Economics and an LLB from the University of Johannesburg, and his earlier career spanned top law firms (Hogan Lovells, Cliffe Dekker Hofmeyr) and in-house legal counsel at Discovery. Based in Johannesburg, he also co-founded and scaled an international photographic safari company.
company_info: TecEx is a global trade-compliance specialist and the market leader in Importer of Record (IOR) and Exporter of Record (EOR) solutions for the technology hardware supply chain. Per its homepage, it removes barriers to trade by handling customs clearance, HS-code classification, regulatory compliance and delivered-duty-paid (DDP) door-to-door logistics so companies can move IT and networking gear across borders to 200+ destinations without a local entity. The company has completed 75,000+ shipments across 211 borders and serves 23 industries.
company_goals: TecEx is building out a single-point-of-contact, technology-enabled trade platform (its TecEx App for instant quotes, shipment tracking and a partner portal) plus value-adds like liability cover, VAT recovery and return-merchandise authorization to derisk and streamline global tech rollouts. It targets enterprises deploying high-value, mission-critical IT, GPU and cloud/data-center hardware globally, and is expanding into emerging digital markets such as East African data centers. The goal is hassle-free, fully compliant global deployment for any business shipping tech hardware.
funding_notes: Bootstrapped global Importer-of-Record/customs-compliance firm (~$68.6M 2026 revenue); no disclosed external funding rounds.

title: CEO
person_info: Moshe Tanach is the founder and CEO of NeuReality, which he started in Sep 2018 to design next-generation AI inference infrastructure. He is a semiconductor and systems veteran with prior senior architecture and R&D leadership roles at Marvell (Director of Architecture/ASIC), Intel (WiFi/Bluetooth product line director), DesignArt Networks and Siano Mobile Silicon, and holds a B.Sc. from the Technion. He is based in Israel and describes himself as an entrepreneur and a dreamer.
company_info: NeuReality is an AI infrastructure-optimization company focused on production AI inference at scale. Per its homepage, it helps AI infrastructure teams maximize GPU/XPU throughput, reduce latency and lower cost per token, framing modern AI deployments as 'token factories.' Its core products are NR-NEXUS (an 'Inference OS' that orchestrates, routes, optimizes, monitors and governs inference across models, runtimes, tenants and heterogeneous compute), the NR2 AI-SuperNIC networking silicon, and an AI-CPU that combines compute, networking and orchestration on a single chip.
company_goals: NeuReality is building the software and silicon stack to turn fragmented inference into governed production capacity, improving utilization, protecting SLOs and cutting cost-per-token without locking customers into one accelerator, cloud, model or serving framework. It offers NR-NEXUS via serverless API, managed deployment, or annual license, and its NR2 AI-SuperNIC (1.6 Tbps, ultra-low latency, UEC support) targets scale-out GPU/XPU clusters and giga-factories. Customers are AI infrastructure operators, neoclouds, and platform/enterprise teams running large-scale production inference.
valuation: ~$86.7M (last disclosed) to ~$175M enterprise value (varies by source)
key_investors: OurCrowd, Varana Capital, Cardumen Capital, SK Hynix, Xilinx, European Innovation Council (EIC)
funding_notes: Israeli AI inference/datacenter chip startup (founded 2019); total funding cited as $63M-$70M across sources.

title: SVP
person_info: Anna Grigoryeva is a Senior Vice President at PSG Equity (since Nov 2020), based in London, focused on early and growth-stage B2B technology investments across Europe, the US and Israel. Her deals include Frisbii (ex Billwerk), Visualfabriq, Commbox, Whoz, ZeroNorth, N2F (exited to FTV Capital), Hostaway, Corilus and Emotion Mobility. Originally from Portland, Oregon (BSc, University of Oregon), she previously invested at Karma Ventures and worked in portfolio operations at Project A Ventures, with board-observer roles at Infermedica and Minut.
company_info: PSG Equity is a growth equity firm that partners with middle-market and growth-stage software and technology-enabled services companies, investing across Europe, the US and Israel with roughly $30bn in AUM. Per its homepage, the firm positions itself on operational experience in the growth-stage software space, helping companies at their inflection point scale through a 'tried and tested growth engine.' Portfolio companies span IT monitoring (LogicMonitor), cybersecurity (Hornetsecurity, Glasswall), GRC (Protecht, CENTRL), healthcare software (Ensora Health) and fintech (SavvyMoney).
company_goals: PSG is working to identify and scale leading software businesses, deploying capital plus operational and strategic support (including M&A roll-ups and international expansion) to help them unlock growth, expansion and impact. It serves founders and management teams of B2B software and tech-enabled-services companies, typically at the growth inflection point, and recently raised PSG Europe II at over EUR 2.6bn to invest exclusively in European software. It markets itself as a founder-friendly, collaborative partner for sustained scaling.
latest_round: Closed two funds totaling $8B (PSG VI $6B + PSG Sequel $2B), Feb 2025
key_investors: LPs: CPP Investments, GIC, StepStone, Hamilton Lane
funding_notes: Growth equity firm (founded 2014); these are fund commitments it manages, not capital it raised as a startup.

title: Account Executive
person_info: Phil Sheaff is an Account Executive (and solutions-oriented technical seller) at Netris, based in the Greater Asheville, NC area, where he focuses on selling the company's AI cloud networking platform to neocloud and AI infrastructure operators. He brings 15+ years in IT as a senior network engineer (including a prior role at Dell Technologies) and is a Cisco Certified Network Associate (CCNA). Earlier in his career he ran his own network-engineering and audio/video consultancy and worked as a music producer, holding a BA in Music Synthesis from Berklee College of Music.
company_info: Netris (Netris, Inc., founded 2017, HQ Santa Clara, CA) is a software company that provides the leading network automation and multi-tenancy platform for AI cloud and GPU infrastructure operators. Per its homepage, Netris is the foundational, cloud-provider-grade networking layer across the entire AI stack (Ethernet, InfiniBand, NVLink, DPUs, virtual and edge networking), giving operators AWS-style abstractions (VPCs, elastic IPs, load balancers) plus hardware-level multi-tenancy and isolation. Engineers define the desired end state and Netris auto-configures VRFs, VXLANs, BGP and more in under two minutes with closed-loop assurance.
company_goals: Netris's mission is to 'turn GPUs into revenue' by removing the networking bottleneck for AI clouds, letting operators launch GPU clouds in weeks, onboard tenants in minutes with hard isolation, maximize GPU utilization and avoid building fragile in-house automation. It serves neoclouds, NVIDIA Cloud Partners, AI factories, sovereign AI operators and enterprises, partnering with NVIDIA, HPE, Mirantis, Rafay, Spectro Cloud and vCluster. The company reports rapid growth (800% ARR, 20+ live deployments) and recently raised a Series A led by Andreessen Horowitz to accelerate adoption worldwide.
key_investors: Andreessen Horowitz (a16z, lead), Berkeley SkyDeck, Alumni Ventures, AAL Management
funding_notes: Network automation/multi-tenancy for AI infrastructure; a16z-led Series A after 800% ARR growth.

title: Co-founder & CEO
person_info: Majid Yazdani is co-founder and CEO of Leeroo (since Aug 2023), a London-based, YC-backed AI startup. He is an AI researcher and engineer whose goal is to model intelligence and make high-quality AI accessible, and was previously a staff scientist at Meta AI (co-developing 'Neural Databases') and at LinkedIn AI (content understanding and economic-graph representation learning), with earlier roles spanning VP at BYJU'S, the University of Geneva and the Idiap Research Institute. He holds a PhD from EPFL.
company_info: Leeroo is a YC-backed (S25) AI company based in London building Uniforge, an autonomous platform that builds enterprise context for AI agents. Per its homepage, Uniforge connects fragmented enterprise data (CRM, billing, ticketing, communications, data warehouses) and autonomously resolves entity identities, discovers relationships, enriches from the web, ensures data quality and builds ML models so AI agents stop hallucinating on partial, disconnected data. It also extracts operational knowledge (processes, rules, service definitions, team responsibilities) into a structured, queryable knowledge graph and runs inside the customer's own cloud (AWS, Azure or GCP).
company_goals: Leeroo is building the 'context layer for the AI era,' aiming to deliver complete, current enterprise context (entity resolution, data quality and ML models like churn, fraud and cross-sell) in days rather than months and without a dedicated data team. Its go-to-market starts with a free 48-hour data audit, then a managed setup that stays continuously up to date. It targets enterprises and data/AI teams, with early pilots in banking, finance and healthcare where data sovereignty and on-prem/in-VPC deployment matter.
key_investors: Y Combinator
funding_notes: London-based YC-backed AI agents startup (founded 2024); only a pre-seed round, amount not publicly disclosed.

title: Co-founder, CFO
person_info: Richard Wang is co-founder and CFO of DensityAI, an AI hardware startup founded by alumni of Tesla's Dojo supercomputer program. A Stanford Graduate School of Business alum based in the San Francisco Bay Area, his background is in finance and investing, with prior roles at Tenzing Global Investors, Sculptor Capital Management, The Pritzker Organization and Millennium.
company_info: DensityAI is building a new generation of AI accelerators around a novel near-memory compute architecture aimed specifically at frontier-scale large language model inference. Its homepage explains the chip combines the near-memory compute topology of SRAM designs with the density of HBM, with the goal of beating GPUs on both energy and speed for large-model, long-context workloads. The company is designing a full-stack solution from silicon to rack to software, and is based in Mountain View, CA.
company_goals: DensityAI is engineering the die, package and system layers together to push past the memory wall, targeting compute utilization of 90%+ versus roughly 7% on HBM GPUs. It is selling the world's fastest inference compute platform for frontier models to operators of large-scale AI/datacenter inference, and is hiring across silicon, packaging, networking and software as it scales out of stealth.
funding_notes: AI-accelerator/datacenter startup founded 2025 by ex-Tesla Dojo lead Ganesh Venkataramanan and ~20 ex-Dojo engineers; still in stealth, funding not publicly disclosed.

title: Head of Developer Relations
person_info: Darin Verheijke leads Developer Relations at Featherless AI, where he evangelizes the platform to developers and speaks at AI events (e.g. GenAI Zurich 2026, where he gave the talk 'Why Specialized Models Will Always Matter'). Based in Belgium and educated at Universiteit Antwerpen, he is an active community-facing voice on the economics of moving teams from frontier models toward smaller, purpose-built specialized models.
company_info: Featherless AI is a serverless AI inference platform whose goal is to make every open-weight model available for instant, serverless inference. Per its homepage and docs, it offers one-click access to 30,000+ open-source models (Llama, Qwen, Mistral, DeepSeek, GLM, RWKV and more) via API with no infrastructure to manage, and is an official inference provider on Hugging Face.
company_goals: Featherless is building toward democratizing AI by making the world's largest open-model catalog accessible without managing infrastructure, token limits or cost creep, offering serverless inference plus agent runtimes. It targets developers, creators, role-play/creative-writing users and businesses scaling production AI apps, and markets dramatically lower inference costs (over 10x cheaper) along with research into non-transformer architectures and reliable web-task AI agents.
key_investors: AMD Ventures, Airbus Ventures (co-leads), BMW i Ventures, Kickstart Ventures, Panache Ventures, Wavemaker Ventures
funding_notes: Serverless open-source AI inference platform (founded 2023/24); $5M seed (Mar 2025) + $20M Series A (Apr 2026).

title: Strategic Account Executive
person_info: Andy Griffin is a Strategic Account Manager/Executive at vCluster (formerly Loft Labs), focused on Kubernetes for GPU, AI and HPC workloads. Based in London, he brings an enterprise software sales background with prior roles at HashiCorp, Cloudera, Moogsoft and SAP.
company_info: vCluster (vCluster Labs) builds the next-generation virtualization layer for Kubernetes: virtual, fully isolated Kubernetes 'tenant clusters' that run inside a host cluster or standalone on bare metal/VMs. Per its homepage, it gives every tenant their own CNCF-certified Kubernetes cluster with a completely virtualized, invisible control plane, delivering hyperscaler-grade isolation built for AI cloud providers, AI factories and multi-cloud Kubernetes platforms.
company_goals: vCluster is building Kubernetes multi-tenancy and tenant isolation for production AI infrastructure - including products like vNode for secure bare-metal/GPU tenant isolation and Auto Nodes/Standalone for dedicated setups. It serves GPU clouds, regulated and sovereign platforms, and enterprises (used by NVIDIA, NScale and 50+ GPU clouds plus Fortune 500s), helping them provision isolated clusters fast while cutting cost and cluster sprawl.
key_investors: Khosla Ventures, Fusion Fund, Surface Ventures, Emergent Ventures, Berkeley SkyDeck Fund
funding_notes: Operated by Loft Labs; creators of open-source virtual Kubernetes clusters (vCluster).

title: Head of Data Centers
person_info: Dr. Manesh Patel (PhD, CEng, MIET) is Head of Data Centres at Radiant, focused on building the physical infrastructure backbone of the AI economy. UK-based and educated at Swansea University, he writes extensively on AI factory design, efficiency (PUE/PUE+), and the land-power-connectivity bottlenecks of deploying AI infrastructure at scale.
company_info: Radiant (radiant.co) describes itself as the first fully integrated AI infrastructure platform, building and operating the 'AI Factories' behind the AI era - unifying software, power, land, capital and compute into one vertically integrated utility model. Backed by Brookfield (100B+ in deployable capital) and an NVIDIA Cloud Partner using Blackwell/Rubin GPUs, it operates a large powered-land portfolio (5 GW live, 45 GW of renewable capacity) to deploy AI compute at speed.
company_goals: Radiant is building and operating data centers from 'shovel to model,' offering a full AI Cloud (inference, fine-tuning, model registry, serverless Kubernetes, high-performance storage) on its proprietary dependency-free software platform that scales from 10K to 100K+ GPUs. It serves technology giants, AI builders and nations (including sovereign solutions and strategic infrastructure), aiming to deliver below-market power costs and total infrastructure control.
key_investors: Draper Associates, Boost VC
funding_notes: Nuclear microreactor startup (Radiant Nuclear); $300M+ Series D closed ~6 months after Series C to fund R-50 factory in Oak Ridge, TN.

title: Director of European Government Affairs
person_info: Tessy Antony de Nassau is European Director of Government Affairs at Armada, leading the company's government relations across Europe. A Luxembourgish businesswoman, NATO/UN peacekeeping veteran (Kosovo) and former member of Luxembourg's Grand Ducal Family, she holds an MSc in International Studies and Diplomacy and has a long career in international government affairs, humanitarian advocacy (UNAIDS Global Advocate), impact investing and entrepreneurship.
company_info: Armada is a US-headquartered (San Francisco, founded 2022) edge computing company providing modular, full-stack AI infrastructure. Its platform combines portable 'Galleon' modular data centers, the Commander operating system for managing edge deployments, and Starlink satellite connectivity, enabling customers to process data and run AI in remote or disconnected environments where conventional connectivity and power are unavailable.
company_goals: Armada is building and scaling modular, megawatt-class data centers (e.g. Leviathan, and the Galleon Forge One factory in Arizona with Johnson Controls) to bring AI compute to the edge. Valued at ~$2B after a $230M Series B, it targets defense, energy and industrial customers and governments (including an MOU on the U.S. DOE's Genesis Mission and MENA expansion), with a mission to remove barriers to AI adoption and bridge the digital divide.
key_investors: Founders Fund, Lux Capital, BlackRock, Overmatch, 8090 Industries, M12 (Microsoft), Shield Capital, Johnson Controls
funding_notes: Edge/modular AI data-center company; Series B co-led by Overmatch, BlackRock, 8090 at $2B valuation.

title: VP - Strategic Delivery and Operations Okta
person_info: Stela Llusha is a VP of Delivery Enablement & Operations for the Customer Identity Cloud (Auth0) business at Okta, based in the San Francisco Bay Area, where she has also held a VP of R&D Operations role. An operations and program-management leader, she previously served as VP of Operations at Samsara and held positions at Twilio, Yahoo!, and Accenture, with degrees from UC Berkeley and Rutgers.
company_info: Okta is the self-described "World's Identity Company," providing the Okta (Workforce Identity) and Auth0 (Customer Identity) platforms that secure access, authentication, and automation for human, machine, and AI agent identities. Its homepage positions identity as the foundation of business security and growth, securing Fortune 100s, governments, and major brands such as McLaren, Wyndham, Hitachi, FedEx, and Mars.
company_goals: Okta is building out an identity security fabric and AI-agent governance (e.g., "Okta for AI Agents") to secure the full lifecycle of every identity, with a heavy current focus on securing AI and the "agentic enterprise." It sells workforce identity, customer identity, and identity governance to enterprises, governments, and developers seeking Zero Trust security, passwordless sign-in, fraud/bot prevention, and seamless customer experiences.
latest_round: IPO April 2017 ($187M); ~$229M over 9 rounds pre/at IPO
valuation: Public; ~$1.2B at 2015 private round; NASDAQ:OKTA today
key_investors: Andreessen Horowitz, Sequoia Capital, Greylock Partners
funding_notes: NASDAQ:OKTA identity/access management firm; FY2025 revenue $2.61B; long public.

title: Head of Family Office and Board Member
company: Limak Datacenters Engineering and Construction and Elektromekanik
person_info: Hakan Elmas is Head of the Family Office and a board member associated with Turkey's Limak Group, overseeing investment and operations spanning data centers, construction, and electromechanical (elektromekanik) businesses. His background is in family-office leadership and global-macro hedge-fund management (including a prior VP - Family Office role at MV Holding), with skills across private equity, institutional investment, infrastructure, colocation, hyperscalers, and cloud.
company_info: Limak is one of Turkey's largest international conglomerates (founded 1976), with over 47,000 employees and projects in 14 countries across construction, energy, tourism, cement, infrastructure, food & beverage, and technology. Its construction arm is Turkey's second-largest international contractor, and its energy electrical & mechanical contracting unit delivers mechanical and electrical design-and-build services, while Limak Technology provides IT infrastructure, software, cybersecurity, 24/7 monitoring, and data-center-related digital solutions.
company_goals: Limak is expanding into data-center, cloud infrastructure, and electromechanical engineering alongside its flagship megaprojects (Kuwait International Airport Terminal 2, FC Barcelona's Spotify Camp Nou redevelopment, airports, ports, dams, and renewable energy). It serves governments, hyperscalers, and enterprise clients through engineering, construction, energy generation/distribution, and technology services, while pursuing sustainability and social-impact goals such as its Global Engineer Girls program.
key_investors: Kuwait Finance House, National Bank of Kuwait
funding_notes: Part of Limak Holding, a major Turkish construction/infrastructure/energy conglomerate (founded 1976); the $831M figure was a debt raise, not equity venture funding.

title: Cybersecurity Investor
person_info: Adrien Vanheste is a cybersecurity investor at TIN Capital, where he works as a Venture Partner / investment-team associate sourcing and evaluating deals. He has a cybersecurity-consultant and cyber-strategy background (a finalist in the Wavestone Wavegame cybersecurity tournament and author on cyber-power for the Institut Open Diplomacy), and previously interned in the digital department of the French Ministry of Foreign Affairs.
company_info: TIN Capital is a Dutch venture capital firm founded in 1998 (originally The Informal Investors Network), and is the only Benelux-based investment firm specialized in cybersecurity, making it one of Europe's longest-running specialist cyber VCs. From offices in Amsterdam and The Hague, it invests in European cybersecurity and IoT-security startups and scale-ups, with portfolio companies including BreachLock, Eye Security, EGERIE, SignPath, EclecticIQ, and Saporo.
company_goals: TIN Capital is investing its 80M-euro-plus European Cyber Tech Fund V (backed by the EIF and Invest-NL) into roughly 12 European cybersecurity scale-ups at Series A/B, targeting IoT security, privacy-enhancing tech, national & economic security tech, and cyber-liability insurtech. Its stated mission is to build European cybersecurity "champions" and contribute to a safer, more resilient, and more digitally autonomous Europe amid regulations like NIS2, the Cybersecurity Act, and DORA.
total_funding: Venture capital firm (not a fundraising startup)
latest_round: European Cyber Tech Fund V; +EUR15M committed (2025)
key_investors: Invest-NL, ROM Regio Utrecht, InnovationQuarter (LPs)
funding_notes: Dutch VC firm founded 1998 (formerly TIIN Capital), invests in European cybersecurity/IoT; figures are fund size, not funding raised.

title: Director
person_info: Michael Charles Borrelli is a Director at AI & Partners, based in London, with 10+ years of experience in financial services, compliance, and technology. He helps clients navigate the EU AI Act and AI governance, frequently speaks and publishes on AI regulation, and also serves as an IoD Finance and FinTech Ambassador and an AI 2030 Chapter Advisor.
company_info: AI & Partners is a European AI governance software firm ("AI That You Can Trust") that provides compliance software for global AI laws, with a particular focus on the EU AI Act. Per its site, it builds an AI Governance platform that simplifies regulatory compliance so firms of all sizes can operate within an AI-enabled environment while keeping trust at the core of their business; it was co-founded and is led by CEO Sean Musch.
company_goals: AI & Partners is building and selling an AI governance/compliance platform plus advisory services that map AI systems to the EU AI Act's risk-based framework (prohibited, high-risk, etc.), handle incident reporting, and enable responsible, auditable AI use. It serves enterprises subject to the EU AI Act and related AI regulation, especially in financial services, working toward making trustworthy, compliant AI adoption practical and humanity-beneficial.
funding_notes: Privately held UK AI-governance/EU AI Act compliance consultancy (AI & PARTNERS LTD, Companies House 07971880); no disclosed venture funding.

title: Digital Growth Partner
person_info: Nick Dunayskyy is a Digital Growth Partner at SeoProfy, positioning himself around "ROI-first SEO for SaaS, E-commerce & Legal" and representing the agency at industry events such as Shoptalk Europe 2026 in Barcelona. He focuses on tying organic search and AI-search visibility to pipeline and revenue, treating organic visibility as an engineering rather than a marketing problem.
company_info: SeoProfy is an ROI-driven, data-driven SEO agency founded in 2012 by Victor Karpenko, headquartered in Orlando, Florida, with 100+ specialists serving 250+ clients across 45 countries and 15 languages. Its homepage frames it as a strategic SEO partner that turns organic search into a predictable revenue channel, backed by proprietary in-house tools (SearchAnalytics and LinkChecker) and 13+ years of international SEO experience.
company_goals: SeoProfy sells SEO services including technical SEO, keyword research, content, on-page/off-page SEO, link building, local SEO, site recovery, and managed SEO, plus a growing AI-search offering (AI SEO/AEO, generative engine optimization, ChatGPT SEO). It targets ecommerce, SaaS, and legal clients and is working toward helping brands stay visible across Google and AI/answer engines as generative AI reshapes product discovery, with measurable organic revenue as the core use case.
funding_notes: Bootstrapped SEO/digital marketing agency (Orlando, FL); no VC funding reported, services from ~$1,600/mo.

title: GTM Director Europe
person_info: Orme de Saint Hilaire is Director, GTM Europe at SambaNova Systems (since May 2025), based in London and focused on growing the company's enterprise AI business across EMEA. He has spent roughly a decade helping US tech companies establish in EMEA, including sales leadership roles at DataStax, DataRobot and Trifacta, and earlier founded an award-winning tech startup while studying at Trinity College Dublin.
company_info: SambaNova is an AI hardware and inference company whose homepage positions it as 'the fastest AI inference platform,' delivering high-performance inference, fine-tuning and scalable solutions for agentic AI that integrate into existing data center infrastructure. Its technology centers on purpose-built RDU (Reconfigurable Dataflow Unit) AI chips with a three-tier memory and dataflow architecture, including its fifth-generation SN50 chip, designed for fast, energy-efficient inference on large models.
company_goals: SambaNova is building a full chips-to-model stack (SN40/SN50 RDUs, SambaRack, SambaStack, SambaCloud and OpenAI-compatible APIs) optimized for fast, low-power agentic inference and the ability to run and switch between many frontier open-source models (DeepSeek, Llama, gpt-oss, MiniMax) on a single node. It is pushing 'premium inference' for AI agents and a network of sovereign-AI data center partners worldwide, serving enterprises, governments and research labs that need high-throughput, energy-efficient AI within their own borders.
key_investors: SoftBank Vision Fund 2, Temasek, GIC, BlackRock, Intel Capital, GV, Walden International, WRVI
funding_notes: AI chip/systems startup; Series D made it the best-funded AI systems startup at the time.

title: CEO
person_info: Dr. Nithin Thomas is the founder and CEO of Klarytee, a London-based data-security startup he founded in 2021. A serial entrepreneur with over a decade in the security industry (he previously founded SQR Systems and won the Shell LiveWIRE 2012 Innovation Award), he positions Klarytee as a SaaS platform that builds security directly into sensitive content so control follows the data wherever it goes.
company_info: Klarytee is a British, patent-based 'data-centric governance platform' that gives organisations sovereign control over their data with visibility, protection and control for AI and beyond. Rather than securing the perimeter, it embeds protection and policy into the data fragment itself via selective fragment encryption, cryptographically binding access and usage policies to the data so control persists across vendors, AI pipelines (including RAG) and downstream environments.
company_goals: Klarytee is building fine-grained fragment encryption, data discovery/cataloguing, and continuous data intelligence designed for crypto agility and post-quantum readiness (aligned with NCSC and NIST), governing access for both human users and AI agents on a strict need-to-know basis. Backed by a 700K pre-seed round (Concept Ventures and notable angels) and working with enterprise/government design partners, it targets organisations handling highly sensitive information - investment banking deal teams, regulated enterprises and government - that want to use AI and cloud without losing control of their data.
key_investors: Concept Ventures, Michael Coates (ex-Twitter CISO), Evelyn Bourke (ex-Bupa CEO), founders of Digital Shadows
funding_notes: London-based data-centric security (encryption) SaaS startup founded 2021 by Nithin Thomas.

title: Founder & CEO
person_info: Jean-Luc Vuattoux, PhD, is the founder of Lexyra AI (since Sep 2025), based in Lausanne, Switzerland, building what he calls 'the trust layer for PDFs in agentic AI systems.' A deep-tech entrepreneur with 20+ years in complex document analysis and industrial data processing, he co-founded Eranea (acquired by LzLabs), led engineering teams of up to 45 people, worked as a software architect at AWS on the Mainframe Modernization Application Testing service, and is co-author of five patents.
company_info: Lexyra AI is a cybersecurity/document-intelligence company that analyzes PDFs at the rendering layer to detect hidden text and images before they reach AI agents. Its homepage frames PDFs as 'an unguarded attack surface for agentic AI,' where hidden layers, white-on-white text, microscopic fonts and off-page elements can slip past conventional parsers and guardrails to trigger indirect prompt injection, data leakage or hijacked autonomous workflows.
company_goals: Lexyra is building a rendering-aware trust layer (delivered as REST API, MCP server, CLI, and cloud SaaS or on-premise) that maps 20+ PDF concealment techniques, scores risk, and quarantines or approves documents both on ingestion and on the way out to prevent propagation and covert exfiltration. Its deep-extraction engine also rebuilds complex document structure, hierarchy, links and multi-page tables into clean, AI-ready data for RAG pipelines and enterprise workflows; the MVP is in selected demos as the company opens discussions with design partners and investors.
funding_notes: No direct match found; search surfaced many similarly-named legal-AI firms (Lexic.ai, Lexi, Lexion, Lexroom.ai) but none confirmed as 'Lexyra AI'.

title: Co-Head of Investments
person_info: Yuriy Sheyko leads investments at Acquinox Capital, where he was brought on to develop the firm's direct private equity activity (the firm's site lists him as Investment Manager; the conference lists him as Co-Head of Investments). Based in Luxembourg, his background spans private markets and capital-markets work, including involvement with Baltic Partners and the launch of a 50M+ Baltic Capital Markets Acceleration Fund.
company_info: Acquinox Capital, founded in 2023 and based in Luxembourg/Switzerland, is a technology-focused private equity and advisory firm that empowers investors to access market leaders through curated private investing. It backs 'Next-Gen Industries' across Europe and North America, combining deep sector expertise with rigorous due diligence to source, support and scale tech-driven companies, and also runs a closed-end venture capital fund focused on mobile-first startups at late-seed and Series A stages.
company_goals: Acquinox partners with companies across the full growth spectrum - from early commercialization to pre-IPO maturity - offering not just capital but strategic value-add, and has gained exposure to high-profile names such as Epic Games and xAI. Its thematic focus targets scalable technologies positioned to reshape global industries, serving private-capital investors seeking curated, tech-driven private-market opportunities; it published a 'Discovering Next-Gen Industries' whitepaper articulating this deep-tech investment thesis.
funding_notes: Luxembourg/Zurich private-equity & VC firm (founded 2023); it is an investor (closed-end VC fund), fund size not publicly disclosed.

title: VP
person_info: Daniel Beerstecher is a VP at MGX, the Abu Dhabi technology investment firm, based in the Abu Dhabi Emirate. His investment background includes roles as an Associate at Hg Capital and prior experience at Sun Capital and Morgan Stanley, and he is a graduate of ESADE Business & Law School.
company_info: MGX is an Abu Dhabi-based global technology investment company launched in 2024 by sovereign wealth fund Mubadala and AI firm G42, chaired by Sheikh Tahnoon bin Zayed Al Nahyan. Built specifically for the AI era, it invests across the entire AI value chain - AI infrastructure (data centers and connectivity), semiconductors, and AI core technologies, models and applications - and has positioned itself less like a traditional Gulf sovereign fund and more like a global asset manager that raises and invests outside capital.
company_goals: MGX is deploying capital at massive scale (it closed a fund of nearly $50B in 2026 and targets $100B+ in AUM), having taken stakes in OpenAI, Anthropic and xAI, co-led major AI rounds, and backed infrastructure vehicles alongside BlackRock, Microsoft and Nvidia (including a $30B data-center fund) plus projects like Europe's largest AI campus in France. Its goal is to accelerate the development and adoption of AI and advanced technologies globally while cementing Abu Dhabi as a leading hub, serving as a partner/co-investor to frontier AI model companies, chipmakers and data-center operators worldwide.
total_funding: Government body / sovereign-backed fund (~$50B fund raised; targeting $100B AUM)
key_investors: Mubadala, G42 (founding partners); regional sovereign wealth funds, global pension/institutional investors
funding_notes: Abu Dhabi state-owned AI investment firm (est. 2024); an investor itself - backs OpenAI, Anthropic, xAI.

title: Co-Founder
person_info: Russ Vetrano is an accomplished global sales executive (Clarkson University, Computer Science/Math) based in the Raleigh-Durham area, associated with AIxBlock as a co-founder/go-to-market leader. His career spans enterprise sales and revenue leadership roles at AI and GPU-compute companies including Exabits.ai, ElectrifAi and Defined.ai, where he focused on AI data, models and GPU compute strategy. He is a frequent voice on GPU compute and AI infrastructure at industry events such as SuperAI.
company_info: AIxBlock (aixblock.io) is an enterprise AI training-data company that supplies voice, audio and text training data at scale for speech AI and large language models, across 100+ languages with a global crowd of professionals. The company has 7+ years of experience delivering large-scale data projects for Fortune 100 firms and unicorns such as Oracle, AWS, Apple, Uber and Uniphore, and is backed by the EU Innovation Program (ISO 27001 / SOC 2). It also operates a self-hosted AI development platform so clients can connect their own storage and keep full data sovereignty.
company_goals: AIxBlock is building end-to-end training-data services (speech collection/transcription, sound/environment audio, RLHF and LLM fine-tuning datasets, and an off-the-shelf call-center audio library) plus a self-hosted platform with a data engine, training and GPU marketplace. It is working toward being a privacy-first, sovereignty-focused data infrastructure partner for regulated sectors like banking, healthcare and government, differentiating on never retaining client data. Target customers are foundation-model labs and enterprise AI/product teams needing multilingual, production-grade data.
key_investors: European Union (Smart&Start Italia / Invitalia grant)
funding_notes: Delaware-based decentralized AI startup; EUR1.5M EU grant secured plus up to EUR61.5M pre-approved (non-dilutive, under review).

title: Head of AI research
person_info: Saad EL BABIDI is a France-based engineer/consultant who graduated from Centrale Lille (also Ecole polytechnique de Bruxelles and Vrije Universiteit Brussel) with a multidisciplinary background spanning strategy consulting (McKinsey), supply chain, urban mobility (MOBI Electromobility Research Centre), construction and AI (Danone). He works at the intersection of AI, data and strategy, with a strong interest in transport systems and applied AI. (Note: his public profile centers on mobility/consulting/research; his specific 'Head of AI research' role at SPIE could not be independently confirmed from public sources.)
company_info: SPIE (spie.com) is the independent European leader in multi-technical services in the fields of energy and communications, with around 55,000 employees. The group designs, builds, operates and maintains energy-efficient, environmentally friendly facilities, and supports customers through the energy, digital and industrial transitions. It operates across four strategic markets: Smart City, E-fficient Buildings, Industry Services and Energies.
company_goals: SPIE is working to enable a post-carbon society, positioning itself as a key player in decarbonisation, the sustainable transformation of cities and territories, the digitalisation of services and the shift to Industry 4.0. It is increasingly deploying generative AI internally (e.g. an 'AI for all' tool at SPIE ICS) to support engineering and operations. Its customers are industrial, public-sector and infrastructure operators across Europe needing electrical, mechanical, ICT, facility-management and transmission/distribution services.
key_investors: Bpifrance (Lac1 fund holds ~5%); historically PAI Partners
funding_notes: SPIE SA is publicly listed (Euronext Paris); European leader in multi-technical services with ~EUR 8.7B revenue (2023), not venture-funded.

title: Head of Techology and R&D
person_info: Alex Presani is a technology leader who joined AgorAI (the Trieste-based AgorAI Innovation Hub) as Head of Technology and R&D, returning to the city where he grew up. A proud American, he spent roughly twenty years working abroad across Amsterdam, Geneva and New York, and lectures on AI as a geostrategic choice (e.g. at Politecnico di Milano's Executive Master in AI). He frames AI around concrete applications, ecosystems over silos, and ethics-by-design.
company_info: AgorAI Innovation Hub S.p.A. (agorai.it) is a Trieste, Italy-based applied- and basic-research center for data science and advanced AI, launched in 2025 and housed in the historic Palazzo Carciotti. It is a public-private ecosystem backed by partners including the Friuli Venezia Giulia region, Generali, Banca Generali, illycaffè and Fincantieri, in collaboration with Goldman Sachs, Deloitte, Google Cloud and research institutions such as SISSA, ICTP, IIT, MIB Trieste and the universities of Trieste and Udine. Its tagline is 'Where Innovation Serves Humanity'.
company_goals: AgorAI aims to become a leading European hub for applied and frontier AI research, training and startup development - a 'European path to AI' focused on real-world, production-grade systems for industrial and regulated environments with a human-centered, ethics-by-design approach. Its research focuses on health and well-being, regenerative agriculture and food systems, mobility and transport, and finance and capital markets, with Google Cloud providing infrastructure and early access to models like Gemini. It is building a founding technical team (AI Architect, Physical AI and Trustworthy AI research engineers) to serve partner industries and the broader regional innovation economy.
key_investors: Fort Schuyler Ventures, Innova Capital Partners, AI Capital
funding_notes: NY-based AI/data-marketplace startup (founded 2017, CEO Josh Sutton); minimal disclosed funding. Not to be confused with real-estate 'Agora' ($64M raised).

title: Founder & CEO
person_info: Srinath Reddy Ganta (Ganta Srinath Reddy) is a Hyderabad-based founder and CEO who built DeepAnnotate.ai, an AI data-annotation startup incubated at T-Hub. He holds a Master of Engineering in Computer Science from University College of Engineering, Osmania, and has a background spanning innovation and public-systems advisory roles (Centre for Innovations in Public Systems, T-Hub Kickstart) and education-focused ventures (founder of 'TheAISCHOOL', which he presented at AIJ 2025 in Moscow). He is an active member of Hyderabad's startup and AI-skilling ecosystem.
company_info: DeepAnnotate.ai (deepannotate.ai), based at T-Hub 2.0 in Hyderabad, is an enterprise AI data-annotation and human-data generation company positioned as 'the data partner built for serious AI.' It provides diversified human intelligence for multimodal and physical AI systems, with 50k+ human resources, 24/7 operations across three global sites, and a stated 99.8% QA accuracy. It is trusted by teams from Google AI and Uber AI and offers a guaranteed 3-day/5-business-day pilot.
company_goals: DeepAnnotate.ai is building human data and evaluation pipelines across modalities - emotional voice data, physical/egocentric and teleoperation data, image and text (NER, sentiment, intent, document parsing), video annotation, 3D/LiDAR point clouds, and RLHF/LLM alignment - delivered via a structured brief-to-production pipeline. The company is working toward helping teams ship better agentic, multimodal and frontier AI faster with precision-labeled training data at scale. Its customers are Fortune 500 enterprises and AI startups building computer vision, autonomous systems, speech and LLM/agentic models.
funding_notes: No funding data found for Deepannotate.ai specifically; searches surfaced unrelated AI-data firms (SuperAnnotate, Deep Cognition), suggesting it is likely early-stage/unfunded or undisclosed.

title: NeoCloud Global Client Executive
person_info: Jennifer Vannoy is a Global Client Executive for NeoCloud at NetApp, based in the Dallas-Fort Worth metroplex (educated at University of Missouri-Saint Louis). She manages and grows top-tier enterprise accounts, focusing on AI, cloud and enterprise data-infrastructure initiatives and building strong customer and partner relationships. She holds DeepLearning.AI certification and MEDDICC & Challenger sales certifications.
company_info: NetApp (netapp.com) is an intelligent data-infrastructure company and, by its own description, the first name in data storage for over 30 years. It is the only enterprise-grade storage service embedded into the major public clouds, offering unified data storage across any data, workload and environment (on-premises systems like AFF/AFX/FAS, object/block storage, and cloud storage on AWS, Azure and Google Cloud). It pairs storage with data services for AI, security/cyber resilience, cloud workload management, governance and mobility.
company_goals: NetApp's strategy centers on 'Intelligent Data Infrastructure' - an enterprise-grade data platform built to fuel AI factories and move customers out of AI proof-of-concept purgatory, with products like NetApp AFX (disaggregated storage for enterprise AI) and the AI Data Engine (real-time curation, vectorization and semantic search). It is working toward simplifying complex infrastructure, optimizing cloud, accelerating AI outcomes and fortifying security across hybrid/multi-cloud. Its NeoCloud focus serves AI/cloud service providers and large enterprises (customers include NVIDIA, DreamWorks, Porsche Motorsport and S&P Global) needing scalable data platforms for AI.
funding_notes: Publicly traded data-storage company (NASDAQ: NTAP); founded 1992, multi-billion-dollar revenue.

title: Head of Digital Growth
person_info: Anton Vnukov is Head of Digital Growth at SeoProfy (since April 2025), where he owns the new-business pipeline across Europe, the US and Canada and leads the sales team, having rebuilt qualification and outbound systems (Pipedrive, Clay, n8n, LinkedIn/HeyReach). He brings 25+ years of B2B sales leadership, previously serving as Head of Sales Europe at BAS-IP and Regional Sales Director at Ajax Systems, plus fractional GTM/revenue advisory work for tech and security startups.
company_info: SeoProfy is a data-driven, ROI-focused SEO and digital marketing agency founded in 2012 by Victor Karpenko, headquartered in Orlando, Florida with roots in Kyiv, Ukraine. It is built around revenue (not vanity metrics), running a four-step process of niche research, technical and content SEO, link building and weekly sprint reviews, backed by 100+ specialists and proprietary tools like SearchAnalytics.ai.
company_goals: SeoProfy is building organic-search and AI-search (GEO/LLM-citation) programs that turn traffic into pipeline and revenue, expanding beyond classic SEO into AI SEO services, digital PR, CRO and content. It serves B2B SaaS, eCommerce, legal, healthcare, enterprise and iGaming clients across 45+ countries and 12 languages, positioning to help brands stay visible as discovery shifts to ChatGPT, Perplexity and AI Overviews.
funding_notes: Bootstrapped SEO/digital marketing agency (founded 2012, Miami); ~$14.3M est. annual revenue, no disclosed VC funding.

title: Regional Sales Director, EMEA
person_info: Rene van Oosten is Regional Sales Director Europe at SambaNova (since March 2026), selling fast AI inference infrastructure across EMEA, and is also co-founder of GTM advisory firm Van Oosten Group in Malaga. He has 30+ years of enterprise IT sales and sales-leadership experience, most recently as Regional Director Europe at AI-inference chipmaker Groq and EMEA director for cyber-risk firm Squalify, with a track record of strong revenue growth across startups and large IT companies.
company_info: SambaNova Systems is a full-stack AI platform and custom-silicon company founded in 2017 in Palo Alto, building purpose-built AI chips on a Reconfigurable Dataflow Unit (RDU) architecture as an alternative to GPUs. Its homepage positions it around premium AI inference, offering the SN50 fifth-generation RDU chip, SambaRack systems, the SambaStack chips-to-model platform and SambaCloud for fast inference on open-source models like DeepSeek, Llama, gpt-oss and MiniMax.
company_goals: SambaNova is building the fastest, most energy-efficient inference stack for agentic AI, maximizing tokens-per-watt and enabling multiple frontier-scale models to run on a single node, integrated into existing data centers. It targets enterprises, government and sovereign-AI data center partners worldwide (Australia, Europe, UK), and is pushing disaggregated GPU+RDU inference for AI agents while pursuing premium-inference deployments.
key_investors: SoftBank Vision Fund 2, Temasek, GIC, BlackRock, Intel Capital, GV, Walden International, WRVI
funding_notes: AI chip/systems startup; Series D made it the best-funded AI systems startup at the time.

title: Data Center Development
person_info: Jong Hae Choi, Ph.D. works in data center / business development at Incheon International Airport Corporation (IIAC) and has helped lead the airport's Airport AI Alliance (AAA) engagement, including hosting the AAA Leadership Forum in Incheon (June 2026). He also serves as an adjunct professor teaching Air Freight Logistics at Inha University's Graduate School of Logistics, and is active in air-cargo and aviation-AI circles, having published research on airport commercial revenue and big-data-driven gate allocation.
company_info: Incheon International Airport Corporation (IIAC) is a South Korean government-owned enterprise founded in 1999 that constructs, manages, operates and maintains Incheon International Airport (ICN), the primary gateway for the Seoul capital area and one of the world's busiest airports. Beyond running the airport, it develops surrounding areas and provides airport construction/operation consulting and overseas airport projects.
company_goals: IIAC is expanding hub capacity (the Phase 4 expansion, record passenger throughput) while pursuing international growth as an airport investor and operator across projects in Poland (CPK), Indonesia, Kuwait and beyond. It is investing heavily in smart-airport and AI-driven innovation, including data center development and Airport AI Alliance membership, serving passengers, airlines, cargo operators and global airport partners.
funding_notes: State-owned South Korean government agency (founded 1999) operating Incheon International Airport; not venture-funded.

title: Founder
person_info: Sajan Khosa is a founder/operator of Liquid Energy, the AI data center company (listed as Chief Operating Officer on the company's own team page, alongside CEO Sid Ravikumar and President Arsh Sekhon). He helps lead the Kitchener, Ontario-based startup's compute operations and go-to-market for its modular, liquid- and air-cooled AI infrastructure.
company_info: Liquid Energy is a US- and Canada-based data center operator (founded 2023, headquartered in Kitchener, Ontario) that develops turnkey green data centers with advanced AI infrastructure. Its homepage frames it as building 'the gigafactory for AI compute,' using a proprietary thermal/cooling stack, factory-built modular MW compute blocks and grid-ready siting beside solar, wind and natural-gas power to deploy high-density GPU clusters in weeks rather than years.
company_goals: Liquid Energy is building rapidly deployable, infinitely scalable, low-cost AI compute, claiming up to 85% lower cooling costs, zero water consumption and ~60% lower data center build-out capex via its proprietary thermal management. It targets AI developers, hyperscale operators and enterprises needing GPU capacity, distributing via direct commitments, brokers and compute marketplaces with SLA-backed 24/7 operations.
key_investors: Velocity Incubator, F4 Fund (early backers)
funding_notes: Cleantech/AI-compute startup (Toronto/Kitchener & SF, founded late 2023) building modular liquid-cooled GPU datacenters; no funding amount disclosed. Many unrelated 'Liquid Energy' entities exist (LNG, pipelines).

title: Chief Marketing Officer
person_info: Meredith Maines is listed as Chief Marketing Officer at TRIEDGE Investments. Note: public sources do not confirm this specific role and TRIEDGE's own team page does not list a CMO by this name; the most prominent public Meredith (Kendall) Maines is a Stanford-educated marketing/communications leader (ex-Hulu, ex-Google, former first Marketing Partner at Lightspeed Venture Partners, later Chief Communications Officer for the Duke and Duchess of Sussex/Archewell), so the LinkedIn match is left blank to avoid misattribution.
company_info: TRIEDGE Investments is a New York City-based single-family office founded in 2024 by CEO Simcha Hyman (who previously led the Portopiccolo Group), with offices in New York and Tel Aviv and roughly 38 employees. Per its homepage, it is focused on building enduring value across healthcare, financial services, cybersecurity, defense technology and beyond, taking a multi-strategy approach spanning private equity, venture capital, real estate and company incubation.
company_goals: TRIEDGE positions itself as both investor and builder, aiming to be a leading value investor in private healthcare and technology-enabled platforms, recently leading a $52M seed round in healthcare-AI startup Arbiter and a $50M round in private-markets startup Hypha. It partners with founders across stages and verticals, and pairs commercial investing with impact/philanthropy focused on mental health, education, and combating poverty and antisemitism.
funding_notes: Multi-strategy family office (founded 2024 by Simcha Hyman; NYC & Tel Aviv); it deploys capital (e.g. Arbiter's $52M seed) rather than raising it.
